_meta.title: GTCI 2025 — Ministry & Source Intelligence Map
_meta.description: All 77 GTCI indicators mapped to responsible Brunei ministries/agencies and the external data sources GTCI uses to obtain scores. Includes data type classification (Survey / Hard Data / Index / Composite).
_meta.note: Ministry assignments reflect primary policy responsibility. Multiple ministries listed where joint oversight applies. Data types based on GTCI Methodology Report 2025.

data_types.SURVEY: Perception or opinion survey (e.g. WEF Executive Opinion Survey, IMD World Competitiveness Survey)
data_types.HARD_DATA: Official statistics, administrative data, or measured empirical figures
data_types.INDEX: Sub-index or composite score derived from another ranked index
data_types.COMPOSITE: GTCI's own computed composite from multiple sub-components

ministries.name: Ministry of Education
ministries.description: Responsible for formal education, higher education, lifelong learning, and research & innovation indicators.

ministries.name: Ministry of Finance and Economy
ministries.description: Responsible for economic competitiveness, business environment, financial infrastructure, labour market regulation, and economic openness indicators.

ministries.name: Ministry of Transport and Infocommunications
ministries.description: Responsible for ICT infrastructure, digital connectivity, broadband access, and e-government indicators.

ministries.name: Ministry of Home Affairs
ministries.description: Responsible for immigration, personal safety, rule of law, and civil liberties indicators.

ministries.name: Ministry of Health
ministries.description: Responsible for health outcomes, life expectancy, and workforce health indicators.

ministries.name: Ministry of Culture, Youth and Sports
ministries.description: Responsible for quality of life, social cohesion, tolerance, and cultural openness indicators.

ministries.name: Prime Minister's Office
ministries.description: Responsible for governance quality, institutional effectiveness, and national strategy indicators.

ministries.name: Ministry of Development
ministries.description: Responsible for infrastructure development, environmental sustainability, and urban planning indicators.

ministries.name: Multi-Ministry / Cross-Cutting
ministries.description: Indicators that span multiple ministries and require inter-agency coordination.

data_sources.name: WEF Executive Opinion Survey
data_sources.description: Annual survey of business executives assessing institutional quality, business environment, and governance. Conducted by the World Economic Forum.
data_sources.submission_mechanism: Survey responses submitted via WEF country partner (in Brunei: BCCI / AMBD). Brunei must increase business executive respondent base.
data_sources.brunei_contact: Brunei Darussalam National Business Council / AMBD

data_sources.name: IMD World Competitiveness Yearbook
data_sources.description: Annual competitiveness assessment combining hard data (2/3) with executive survey (1/3). Covers 64 economies.
data_sources.submission_mechanism: Hard data submitted by national authorities; survey via IMD partner institutions. Brunei participates through DEPS.

data_sources.name: World Bank — Worldwide Governance Indicators
data_sources.description: Six composite governance dimensions: Voice & Accountability, Political Stability, Government Effectiveness, Regulatory Quality, Rule of Law, Control of Corruption.
data_sources.submission_mechanism: Composite from 30+ underlying sources. Brunei can influence via improving source data and national statistics quality.

data_sources.name: UNESCO Institute for Statistics
data_sources.description: Official education statistics including gross enrolment ratios, literacy rates, government expenditure on education, and pupil-teacher ratios.
data_sources.submission_mechanism: Data submitted by Ministry of Education annually to UNESCO. Brunei must ensure timely and complete submission of education administrative data.

data_sources.name: ILO — ILOSTAT Database
data_sources.description: International Labour Organization statistics on labour force participation, unemployment, wages, and working conditions.
data_sources.submission_mechanism: Data submitted via Department of Labour / Department of Economic Planning & Statistics through ILO STAT database protocols.

data_sources.name: ITU — ICT Statistics
data_sources.description: International Telecommunication Union data on broadband access, mobile subscriptions, internet penetration, and ICT infrastructure.
data_sources.submission_mechanism: Data submitted by AITI / MTIC annually through ITU national data reporting questionnaire.
data_sources.brunei_contact: Authority for Info-communications Technology Industry (AITI) / MTIC

data_sources.name: World Bank — Human Capital Index
data_sources.description: Composite index measuring outcomes in health and education that a child born today can expect to attain by age 18.
data_sources.submission_mechanism: Composite derived from health and education statistical submissions. Brunei must improve data completeness to MOH/UNESCO pipelines.
data_sources.brunei_contact: Ministry of Health / Ministry of Education

data_sources.name: OECD — PISA / Education at a Glance
data_sources.description: Programme for International Student Assessment and Education at a Glance statistics. Brunei participates in PISA.
data_sources.submission_mechanism: PISA: students sit standardised tests coordinated by MoE. Education at a Glance: data submitted to OECD by MoE. Brunei participates but should increase PISA participant cohort.
data_sources.brunei_contact: Ministry of Education — Curriculum Development Department

data_sources.name: Transparency International — CPI
data_sources.description: Corruption Perceptions Index — composite of expert assessments and surveys on public sector corruption.
data_sources.submission_mechanism: Derived from 13 underlying sources. Brunei influenced by improving ACB visibility, public reporting, and participation in underlying source surveys.

data_sources.name: Freedom House — Freedom in the World / Press
data_sources.description: Annual assessments of political rights, civil liberties, and press freedom.
data_sources.submission_mechanism: Expert-assessed. Brunei's score reflects legal framework, civil liberties constraints. Limited direct submission pathway — improvement requires policy reform.

data_sources.name: Global Innovation Index
data_sources.description: WIPO composite index on innovation inputs and outputs including R&D, patents, and knowledge creation.
data_sources.submission_mechanism: Composite from national R&D statistics, patent data, and publication data. Brunei must submit R&D expenditure to WIPO and increase patent filings.
data_sources.brunei_contact: Ministry of Finance and Economy / DEPS / Research & Innovation Agencies

data_sources.name: IMD World Talent Ranking
data_sources.description: IMD ranking on talent investment, appeal, and readiness for business environment.
data_sources.submission_mechanism: Mix of hard data and executive surveys. Submitted via DEPS and IMD partner.

data_sources.name: WHO — Global Health Observatory
data_sources.description: World Health Organization statistics including life expectancy, health expenditure, and disease burden.
data_sources.submission_mechanism: Data submitted by Ministry of Health annually via WHO Country Health Information reporting system.
data_sources.brunei_contact: Ministry of Health — Health Information Unit

data_sources.name: World Bank — World Development Indicators
data_sources.description: Comprehensive cross-country statistics on economic development, trade, and social outcomes.
data_sources.submission_mechanism: Drawn from national statistical reporting pipelines. DEPS is primary submission point. Requires consistent GDP, trade, and population data reporting.
data_sources.brunei_contact: DEPS / Ministry of Finance and Economy

data_sources.name: World Justice Project — Rule of Law Index
data_sources.description: Annual assessment of rule of law across 8 factors including constraints on government, fundamental rights, and civil justice.
data_sources.submission_mechanism: Household and expert surveys conducted in-country. Brunei can request expanded country inclusion or improve survey respondent diversity.

data_sources.name: Economist Intelligence Unit
data_sources.description: EIU Democracy Index and Business Environment assessments.
data_sources.submission_mechanism: Expert-assessed by EIU analysts. Limited direct submission. Brunei can improve via policy reforms and engaging EIU country desk.

indicators.name: Regulatory Quality
indicators.source_name: World Bank Worldwide Governance Indicators — Regulatory Quality
indicators.submission_notes: WGI composite derived from underlying sources including WEF EOS, IMD WCY, and others. Brunei should focus on improving business regulation quality scores in WEF and IMD surveys.
indicators.brunei_challenge: Regulatory environment perceived as overly bureaucratic; e-licensing improvements needed

indicators.name: Rule of Law
indicators.source_name: World Bank Worldwide Governance Indicators — Rule of Law
indicators.submission_notes: WGI composite. Improved judicial independence and contract enforcement perceptions would raise this score.
indicators.brunei_challenge: Perception gap in judicial independence and access to legal remedies

indicators.name: Political Stability
indicators.source_name: World Bank Worldwide Governance Indicators — Political Stability
indicators.submission_notes: One of Brunei's strongest scores. Absolute monarchy and small geographic size contribute to perceived stability.
indicators.brunei_challenge: Maintain current standing

indicators.name: Government Effectiveness
indicators.source_name: World Bank Worldwide Governance Indicators — Government Effectiveness
indicators.submission_notes: Measures quality of public services, civil service, policy formulation. Digital government services expansion would improve this.
indicators.brunei_challenge: Public service delivery speed; e-government adoption lag

indicators.name: Business Sophistication
indicators.source_name: WEF Executive Opinion Survey — Business Sophistication
indicators.submission_notes: Survey-based. Increase number of WEF EOS respondents and promote private sector sophistication through FDI attraction.
indicators.brunei_challenge: Small private sector; limited cluster development; low entrepreneurship scores

indicators.name: Start-up Environment
indicators.source_name: WEF Executive Opinion Survey — Start-up Environment
indicators.submission_notes: Survey-based. Darussalam Enterprise (DARe) programmes and new SME policies could improve executive perception.
indicators.brunei_challenge: Limited venture capital, high regulatory burden for new businesses, small market size

indicators.name: Ease of Hiring Foreign Labour
indicators.source_name: IMD World Competitiveness Yearbook — Labour Regulations
indicators.submission_notes: IMD WCY survey item. Labour market flexibility improvements and streamlined work permit processes would improve score.
indicators.brunei_challenge: Complex work permit process; quota systems; perceived inflexibility

indicators.name: Broadband / Internet Access
indicators.source_name: ITU ICT Statistics — Fixed Broadband Subscriptions per 100 inhabitants
indicators.submission_notes: Hard data submitted to ITU annually. Brunei must increase fixed broadband penetration — current gap vs SG is -26 points.
indicators.brunei_challenge: High mobile internet use but low fixed broadband penetration; infrastructure cost in rural areas

indicators.name: Mobile Broadband Subscriptions
indicators.source_name: ITU ICT Statistics — Mobile Broadband Subscriptions per 100
indicators.submission_notes: Strong performance. Brunei's 5G rollout is underway. Annual ITU reporting through AITI.
indicators.brunei_challenge: Maintain and expand 5G rollout; ensure rural coverage parity

indicators.name: ICT Access
indicators.source_name: ITU ICT Development Index — Access Sub-index
indicators.submission_notes: Composite access score. Improving fixed broadband and household computer access would boost this.
indicators.brunei_challenge: Device affordability and digital skills limiting full ICT access

indicators.name: Income Equality (Gini Coefficient)
indicators.source_name: World Bank World Development Indicators — Gini Index
indicators.submission_notes: Hard data. Brunei's Gini data must be regularly updated and submitted to World Bank. Last published data is from 2015 — urgent update needed.
indicators.brunei_challenge: Data gap — Brunei has not published recent household income survey data

indicators.name: Safety & Security
indicators.source_name: WGI Political Stability & Absence of Violence / WEF EOS Safety
indicators.submission_notes: Strong score. Brunei consistently ranks well on safety perceptions. Maintain focus.
indicators.brunei_challenge: Sustain low crime rates and continue public safety programmes

indicators.name: Quality of Life
indicators.source_name: IMD WCY — Quality of Life Index
indicators.submission_notes: IMD survey composite. Improving cultural amenities, work-life balance, and international connectivity would help.
indicators.brunei_challenge: Limited entertainment/cultural offerings; perceived conservatism; low nightlife/diversity scores

indicators.name: Investment in Education (% of GDP)
indicators.source_name: UNESCO UIS — Government Expenditure on Education as % of GDP
indicators.submission_notes: Hard data. Regular annual submission to UNESCO required. Brunei's education spending is high — data must be current.
indicators.brunei_challenge: Ensure consistent and timely data submission; spending quality vs quantity distinction

indicators.name: Tertiary Education Enrolment
indicators.source_name: UNESCO UIS — Gross Tertiary Enrolment Ratio
indicators.submission_notes: Hard data. Brunei must improve enrolment reporting to include part-time and online students. Expansion of UBD/UTAMA capacity.
indicators.brunei_challenge: Low gross enrolment ratio — many Bruneians study abroad which may not be fully captured

indicators.name: Quality of Management Schools
indicators.source_name: WEF EOS — Quality of Management Schools
indicators.submission_notes: Survey-based. Improving international accreditation of UBD business school and attracting international faculty would help.
indicators.brunei_challenge: UBD business school lacks international rankings; no globally recognised MBA programme

indicators.name: Open Immigration Policies
indicators.source_name: WEF EOS — Open Immigration Policies
indicators.submission_notes: Survey-based. Brunei's restrictive immigration stance is perceived as a barrier to talent attraction. Talent Pass or Global Talent Visa scheme urgently needed.
indicators.brunei_challenge: Very restrictive permanent residency; limited talent visa pathways; Bruneianisation policy creates perception of closed labour market

indicators.name: Attractiveness for Talented People
indicators.source_name: IMD WCY — Attractiveness for Talented People
indicators.submission_notes: IMD survey. Require systemic improvements to liveability, economic opportunity, and immigration policy.
indicators.brunei_challenge: Limited career opportunities, cultural restrictions, and immigration barriers

indicators.name: Personal Freedom
indicators.source_name: Freedom House — Freedom in the World Index
indicators.submission_notes: Expert-assessed index. Brunei's Syariah law implementation and political constraints result in low scores. Limited direct submission pathway.
indicators.brunei_challenge: Structural: legal framework, civil liberties constraints inherent to governance model

indicators.name: Tolerance — Minority Groups
indicators.source_name: WEF EOS — Tolerance for Ethnic Minorities
indicators.submission_notes: Survey-based perception. Policy communications and multicultural initiatives may help perception scores.
indicators.brunei_challenge: Perceived intolerance from international business community perspective due to legal context

indicators.name: Formal Education — Access
indicators.source_name: UNESCO UIS — Primary / Secondary Net Enrolment Ratio
indicators.submission_notes: Hard data. Brunei has near-universal primary enrolment. Data submission timeliness improvement needed.
indicators.brunei_challenge: Tertiary and TVET enrolment expansion needed

indicators.name: Pupil-Teacher Ratio
indicators.source_name: UNESCO UIS — Pupil-to-Teacher Ratio
indicators.submission_agency: Ministry of Education — Human Resource Division
indicators.submission_notes: Hard data. Teacher recruitment and retention policies would directly improve this ratio.
indicators.brunei_challenge: Teacher shortages in STEM subjects; reliance on expatriate teachers

indicators.name: Quality of Primary Education
indicators.source_name: WEF EOS — Quality of Primary Education
indicators.submission_notes: Survey-based. Implementation of SPN21 improvements and bilingual curriculum reforms would improve perceptions.
indicators.brunei_challenge: SPN21 curriculum still perceived as underperforming vs regional peers; PISA results gap

indicators.name: University Quality
indicators.source_name: WEF EOS — Quality of the Education System
indicators.submission_notes: Survey. UBD international ranking improvements and global partnerships (Oxbridge linkages) would raise perception.
indicators.brunei_challenge: UBD not in top 400 globally; limited postgraduate research output

indicators.name: PISA Scores
indicators.source_name: OECD PISA — Mathematics, Reading, Science Scores
indicators.submission_agency: Ministry of Education — Curriculum Development Department
indicators.submission_notes: Hard data from PISA standardised tests. Brunei participates. Curriculum reform, teacher training, and reading interventions are key.
indicators.brunei_challenge: Below OECD average in all three PISA domains; significant gap vs Singapore

indicators.name: Lifelong Learning
indicators.source_name: WEF EOS — Extent of Staff Training
indicators.submission_notes: Survey. Expansion of APTIS, CPD programs, and micro-credential ecosystem needed.
indicators.brunei_challenge: Limited formal CPD tracking; no national lifelong learning account system

indicators.name: Training and Development
indicators.source_name: IMD WCY — Training and Development
indicators.submission_notes: IMD survey. National skills framework and TVET uplift programme needed.
indicators.brunei_challenge: Private sector training investment is low; insufficient employer-led training

indicators.name: Growth Opportunity — Vocational
indicators.source_name: WEF EOS — Quality of Vocational Training
indicators.submission_notes: Survey. ITB and IBTE reforms, dual apprenticeship models, and industry-linked TVET would improve scores.
indicators.brunei_challenge: TVET still carries social stigma; misalignment with industry needs; low industry participation

indicators.name: Career Development
indicators.source_name: IMD WCY — Employee Training
indicators.submission_notes: IMD survey. National career guidance infrastructure and private sector CPD investment required.
indicators.brunei_challenge: Limited career pathways outside oil & gas and public sector

indicators.name: VTS — Mid-level Skills
indicators.source_name: ILO ILOSTAT — Share of Medium-skilled Workers
indicators.submission_notes: Hard data. Brunei's labour force occupational classification data must be submitted to ILO annually. TVET expansion would shift workforce composition.
indicators.brunei_challenge: Dependence on low-skilled foreign labour; insufficient mid-level skill development

indicators.name: Technicians in R&D
indicators.source_name: UNESCO UIS — Technicians in R&D per million population
indicators.submission_notes: Hard data. Brunei scores zero — data is either not submitted or R&D technician headcount is genuinely near zero. Urgent data collection and submission needed.
indicators.brunei_challenge: Zero score = either data gap or structural absence of R&D technician roles

indicators.name: VTS — Employability
indicators.source_name: WEF EOS — Ease of Finding Skilled Employees
indicators.submission_notes: Survey. Improving TVET-industry linkages and publishing graduate employability statistics would help.
indicators.brunei_challenge: Skills mismatch between education output and labour market demand

indicators.name: Youth Unemployment
indicators.source_name: ILO ILOSTAT — Youth Unemployment Rate (15–24)
indicators.submission_notes: Hard data. Brunei's youth unemployment is structurally high (~22%). Data submitted to ILO via DEPS. Urgent jobs creation and skills matching needed.
indicators.brunei_challenge: Structural youth unemployment; over-dependence on public sector jobs; skills mismatch

indicators.name: Labour Productivity
indicators.source_name: World Bank WDI — GDP per person employed (2017 PPP $)
indicators.submission_notes: Hard data via World Bank. GDP and employment data must be current. Economic diversification drives productivity gains.
indicators.brunei_challenge: O&G sector skews productivity figure; non-oil economy has lower productivity

indicators.name: GAS — High-level Skills
indicators.source_name: ILO ILOSTAT — Share of High-skilled Workers in Employment
indicators.submission_notes: Hard data. High-skilled worker share reflects education outcomes and labour market structure.
indicators.brunei_challenge: Brain drain of high-skilled Bruneians; insufficient high-skill local job opportunities

indicators.name: Critical Thinking in Teaching
indicators.source_name: WEF EOS — Critical Thinking in Teaching
indicators.submission_notes: Survey. Pedagogical reforms in SPN21 to promote inquiry-based learning, Socratic method, and critical thinking are needed.
indicators.brunei_challenge: Rote learning culture persists; teacher training lacks critical thinking methodology

indicators.name: GAS — Talent Impact
indicators.source_name: WEF EOS — Talent Attraction & Retention
indicators.submission_notes: Survey. Cross-cutting indicator requiring coordinated national talent strategy.
indicators.brunei_challenge: No national talent attraction strategy; fragmented ministry coordination

indicators.name: Digital Skills
indicators.source_name: WEF EOS — Digital Skills Among Population
indicators.submission_notes: Survey. AITI's Digital Skills Programme and STEM education expansion would directly improve this score.
indicators.brunei_challenge: Digital literacy levels below regional peers; coding education underrepresented in schools

indicators.name: Labour Market Flexibility
indicators.source_name: WEF EOS — Flexibility of Wage Determination
indicators.submission_notes: Survey. Labour law modernisation and removal of fixed-wage determination constraints needed.
indicators.brunei_challenge: Rigid public sector salary scales; limited performance-based pay in private sector

indicators.name: Life Expectancy
indicators.source_name: WHO Global Health Observatory — Life Expectancy at Birth
indicators.submission_agency: Ministry of Health — Health Information Unit
indicators.submission_notes: Hard data. Brunei's life expectancy (~76 years) is moderate. Non-communicable disease prevention and healthy lifestyle initiatives are key.
indicators.brunei_challenge: High NCD burden (diabetes, hypertension); obesity rates among highest in SEA

indicators.name: Health Expenditure (% of GDP)
indicators.source_name: WHO GHO — Total Health Expenditure as % of GDP
indicators.submission_notes: Hard data. Submitted to WHO via annual National Health Accounts. Increased health budget allocation would improve this.
indicators.brunei_challenge: Relatively low health expenditure; primary care underinvestment vs hospital spending

indicators.name: Environmental Performance
indicators.source_name: Yale EPI / Global Innovation Index — Environmental Performance
indicators.submission_agency: Department of Environment, Parks and Recreation
indicators.submission_notes: Composite index from Yale. Brunei's carbon footprint, deforestation protection, and air quality data feed this.
indicators.brunei_challenge: High per-capita carbon emissions from O&G; limited renewable energy adoption

indicators.name: Happiness / Well-being
indicators.source_name: UN World Happiness Report / WEF EOS Well-being
indicators.submission_notes: Survey-based. Social support systems, economic security, and cultural vibrancy affect this score.
indicators.brunei_challenge: Limited social/cultural diversity; limited employment satisfaction in private sector

indicators.name: Openness — External
indicators.source_name: World Bank WDI — Trade Openness (Exports + Imports as % of GDP)
indicators.submission_notes: Hard data. Brunei's trade openness is driven by O&G exports. Diversification would stabilise and potentially improve this.
indicators.brunei_challenge: Over-reliance on O&G for trade; limited non-oil exports

indicators.name: Brain Gain / Talent Retention
indicators.source_name: IMD WCY — Brain Drain and Talent Retention
indicators.submission_notes: IMD survey. Requires cross-ministry talent retention strategy.
indicators.brunei_challenge: Brain drain to Singapore and UK; limited high-skill private sector jobs

indicators.name: Enabling Environment — Corruption
indicators.source_name: Transparency International — Corruption Perceptions Index
indicators.submission_notes: Composite index. ACB's public reporting and enforcement visibility directly impacts perception scores. Increase WEF EOS respondents who give high ACB visibility.
indicators.brunei_challenge: Perception of cronyism; limited independent audit and accountability mechanisms

indicators.name: FDI Net Inflows
indicators.source_name: World Bank WDI — FDI Net Inflows (% of GDP)
indicators.submission_notes: Hard data. Brunei Darussalam Central Bank and DEPS submit FDI statistics to World Bank. BIFC and BEDB promotion efforts directly influence FDI flows.
indicators.brunei_challenge: Non-O&G FDI is low; regulatory environment not perceived as FDI-friendly

indicators.name: Knowledge-Intensive Employment
indicators.source_name: ILO ILOSTAT — Knowledge-Intensive Employment as % of Total
indicators.submission_notes: Hard data. Zero score likely from data gap — Brunei does not publish detailed occupational classification aligned to ISCO knowledge-intensity definition.
indicators.brunei_challenge: Data gap: ISCO-based occupational survey not submitted; knowledge sector employment not formally tracked

indicators.name: Knowledge Creation — Patents
indicators.source_name: WIPO — Patent Applications per million population
indicators.submission_notes: Hard data submitted to WIPO. Brunei's patent filing rate is near zero. IP awareness campaigns and university research commercialisation are critical.
indicators.brunei_challenge: Near-zero domestic patent filings; limited R&D commercialisation culture

indicators.name: Research & Development Expenditure
indicators.source_name: UNESCO UIS — Gross Domestic Expenditure on R&D (% of GDP)
indicators.submission_notes: Hard data. R&D expenditure as % GDP is ~0.1% — far below OECD average of 2.7%. National R&D investment strategy and industry R&D incentives urgently needed.
indicators.brunei_challenge: Critically low R&D investment; no national R&D spend target; industry does not invest in R&D

indicators.name: Researchers in R&D
indicators.source_name: UNESCO UIS — Researchers in R&D per million population
indicators.submission_notes: Hard data. Very low researcher density. Attracting and retaining PhD-level researchers requires salary parity with industry and career pathways.
indicators.brunei_challenge: Low researcher headcount; limited academic-industry research collaboration

indicators.name: ICT Export Services
indicators.source_name: World Bank WDI — ICT Service Exports (% of total service exports)
indicators.submission_notes: Hard data. Near-zero ICT exports. Development of digital economy and tech sector essential. Bruneian tech startups and BPO industry development needed.
indicators.brunei_challenge: Negligible ICT services export base; limited tech industry development

indicators.name: Control of Corruption
indicators.source_name: World Bank Worldwide Governance Indicators — Control of Corruption
indicators.submission_notes: WGI composite. ACB performance and public sector transparency are key drivers.
indicators.brunei_challenge: Perception of elite capture; limited independent oversight institutions

indicators.name: Female Labour Force Participation
indicators.source_name: ILO ILOSTAT — Female Labour Force Participation Rate
indicators.submission_notes: Hard data. Submitted to ILO via DEPS. Childcare subsidies, flexible work policies, and women's entrepreneurship initiatives would improve this.
indicators.brunei_challenge: Cultural norms and limited childcare options suppress female labour participation

indicators.name: E-Government Index
indicators.source_name: UN E-Government Development Index (UNDESA)
indicators.submission_notes: Index assessed by UNDESA. Brunei must accelerate digital government services, open data portals, and citizen digital participation.
indicators.brunei_challenge: Limited open government data; services not fully digital; e-participation low

indicators.name: STEM Graduates
indicators.source_name: UNESCO UIS — STEM Graduates as % of Total Tertiary Graduates
indicators.submission_notes: Hard data. Brunei must increase STEM enrolment and track graduate outcomes. HED should submit disaggregated field-of-study data.
indicators.brunei_challenge: Low STEM interest; lack of STEM career pathways and industry demand signals

indicators.name: Social Mobility
indicators.source_name: WEF Global Social Mobility Index
indicators.submission_notes: Composite index. Brunei's social mobility is shaped by education access, income distribution, and public service accessibility.
indicators.brunei_challenge: Wawasan-era wealth concentration; limited meritocracy in public sector promotions

indicators.name: Burden of Government Regulation
indicators.source_name: WEF EOS — Burden of Government Regulation
indicators.submission_notes: Survey. Business regulatory reform (e.g. reducing permit requirements, ICTF simplification) would improve this score.
indicators.brunei_challenge: Complex permit processes; slow bureaucracy; multiple ministry touchpoints for business

indicators.name: Vocational Participation Rate
indicators.source_name: UNESCO UIS — TVET Enrolment as % of Secondary Enrolment
indicators.submission_notes: Hard data. TVET enrolment rates must be improved. ITB and IBTE expansion and industry-integrated programs are key.
indicators.brunei_challenge: TVET social stigma; students prefer academic over vocational pathways

indicators.name: Immigration Laws
indicators.source_name: IMD WCY — Immigration Laws
indicators.submission_notes: Survey. Streamlining immigration for skilled workers, and introducing talent/investor visas would directly improve this.
indicators.brunei_challenge: Restrictive PR pathways; limited skilled worker visa flexibility

indicators.name: Cognitive Skills
indicators.source_name: World Bank Human Capital Index — Harmonised Test Scores
indicators.submission_notes: Composite from standardised test performance. PISA scores are key inputs. Curriculum reform is essential.
indicators.brunei_challenge: Below-average PISA performance; lower cognitive skill outcomes vs income peers

indicators.name: Education Equity
indicators.source_name: World Bank HCI — Education Component (Survival and Quality)
indicators.submission_notes: HCI composite. Brunei's survival rate to grade 5 is strong but quality gap vs peers persists.
indicators.brunei_challenge: Learning-adjusted years of schooling below regional peers

indicators.name: Global Connectivity
indicators.source_name: ITU / ICAO — International Connectivity (flight routes + internet bandwidth)
indicators.submission_notes: Hard data. Brunei International Airport connectivity and undersea cable bandwidth both factor in.
indicators.brunei_challenge: Limited international flight routes; dependence on Royal Brunei Airlines; limited data centre infrastructure

indicators.name: Press Freedom
indicators.source_name: Reporters Without Borders — World Press Freedom Index
indicators.submission_notes: Expert-assessed. Structural challenge given media ownership and legal constraints.
indicators.brunei_challenge: State media dominance; limited independent press; Syariah-related content restrictions

indicators.name: Physician Density
indicators.source_name: WHO GHO — Physicians per 10,000 population
indicators.submission_notes: Hard data. Brunei has moderate physician density but relies on expatriate doctors. Medical education expansion and scholarship-to-service bonds would help.
indicators.brunei_challenge: Dependence on foreign medical professionals; PAPRSB Institute medical training capacity is limited

indicators.name: Strength of Auditing & Reporting Standards
indicators.source_name: WEF EOS — Strength of Auditing and Reporting Standards
indicators.submission_notes: Survey. IFRS adoption and robust audit standards would improve perception. AMBD regulatory reform is key.
indicators.brunei_challenge: Limited number of Big 4 audit firms; weak listed company sector

indicators.name: International Experience
indicators.source_name: IMD WCY — International Experience
indicators.submission_notes: Survey. Government scholarship recipients returning with international experience are a positive signal.
indicators.brunei_challenge: Limited private sector promotion of international assignments; brain drain risk

indicators.name: Access to Finance
indicators.source_name: WEF EOS — Ease of Access to Loans
indicators.submission_notes: Survey. SME lending facility expansion and AMBD fintech regulations would help.
indicators.brunei_challenge: Limited access to venture capital; conservative banking sector; limited SME credit

indicators.name: Apprenticeship & Internship
indicators.source_name: WEF EOS — Quality of Vocational Education
indicators.submission_notes: Survey. National apprenticeship framework and employer incentives for taking on interns would help.
indicators.brunei_challenge: No formal national apprenticeship legislation; limited structured internship tracking

indicators.name: Cultural Openness
indicators.source_name: WEF EOS — Cultural Openness
indicators.submission_notes: Survey. International cultural exchanges, arts funding, and diaspora engagement programmes would improve perception.
indicators.brunei_challenge: Perceived cultural conservatism limits talent attraction; limited international cultural events

indicators.name: University-Industry Collaboration
indicators.source_name: WEF EOS — University-Industry Collaboration in R&D
indicators.submission_notes: Survey. UBD-industry partnership programmes, research grants co-funded by industry, and spin-off frameworks would improve this.
indicators.brunei_challenge: Limited structured university-industry research partnerships; low industry R&D culture

indicators.name: Domestic Market Size
indicators.source_name: World Bank WDI — GDP (PPP, billions USD)
indicators.submission_notes: Hard data. Brunei's market size is inherently small (~450k population). ASEAN market integration and digital economy would be multiplier effects.
indicators.brunei_challenge: Structural: small population limits market size; ASEAN integration is mitigating factor

indicators.name: Knowledge Graph — Publications
indicators.source_name: WIPO/Scimago — Scientific Publications per million population
indicators.submission_notes: Hard data from Scimago/Scopus. Brunei's publication output is very low. Research output quotas for academic staff and international co-authorship programmes needed.
indicators.brunei_challenge: Very low academic research publication output; limited PhD research programmes

indicators.name: Pension & Retirement Systems
indicators.source_name: Melbourne Mercer Global Pension Index
indicators.submission_notes: Composite index. TAP reform and supplementary savings vehicle expansion would improve Brunei's score.
indicators.brunei_challenge: TAP contribution rates are low; no voluntary supplementary pension scheme for private sector

indicators.name: Innovation Capacity
indicators.source_name: WEF EOS — Company Capacity for Innovation
indicators.submission_notes: Survey. Innovation ecosystem development (DARe, iCentre, iHub) and R&D tax incentives needed.
indicators.brunei_challenge: Private sector innovation culture is weak; limited tech startups; no major R&D-intensive firms

indicators.name: Entrepreneurship
indicators.source_name: WEF EOS — Entrepreneurial Culture
indicators.submission_notes: Survey. DARe entrepreneurship programmes and risk culture promotion through education needed.
indicators.brunei_challenge: Risk-averse culture; preference for government employment; limited startup funding ecosystem

indicators.name: Physical Infrastructure
indicators.source_name: WEF EOS — Quality of Overall Infrastructure
indicators.submission_notes: Survey. Infrastructure spending quality and connectivity are key. Public transport is a weakness.
indicators.brunei_challenge: Limited public transport; road-centric infrastructure; limited urban density

indicators.name: Compensation of Knowledge Workers
indicators.source_name: IMD WCY — Remuneration in Services Sector
indicators.submission_notes: IMD survey. Linked to labour market competitiveness and talent attraction.
indicators.brunei_challenge: Public sector wages are not competitive with Singapore and HK for senior knowledge workers

indicators.name: Cluster Development
indicators.source_name: WEF EOS — State of Cluster Development
indicators.submission_notes: Survey. Brunei Economic Development Board (BEDB) industrial cluster strategy needs acceleration.
indicators.brunei_challenge: Limited industrial clusters; O&G dominates; no tech, biotech, or fintech cluster

indicators.name: Social Protection
indicators.source_name: ILO — Social Protection Coverage Index
indicators.submission_notes: ILO composite. Brunei's social protection through subsidies is high. Formal social insurance scheme documentation needed.
indicators.brunei_challenge: Social protection data not fully formalised in ILO-compatible statistical categories

indicators.name: Availability of Engineers
indicators.source_name: WEF EOS — Availability of Scientists and Engineers
indicators.submission_notes: Survey. Engineering graduate output and industry absorption rates need to be improved. Board of Engineers Brunei (BEB) data should be more prominently published.
indicators.brunei_challenge: Engineering graduates often move into public sector admin rather than engineering practice

indicators.name: Public Institutions Quality
indicators.source_name: WEF EOS — Public Institution Quality / Trust in Politicians
indicators.submission_notes: Survey. Civil service reform and transparent public service delivery improvements would help.
indicators.brunei_challenge: Limited independent institutional accountability; weak civil society feedback mechanisms

indicators.name: External Openness
indicators.source_name: IMD WCY — External Openness Index
indicators.submission_notes: IMD composite. Trade liberalisation, FTA expansion, and open investment regime would improve score.
indicators.brunei_challenge: Limited FTA coverage; over-reliance on bilateral ASEAN trade; non-oil trade barriers

indicators.name: Adult Literacy
indicators.source_name: UNESCO UIS — Adult Literacy Rate (15+)
indicators.submission_notes: Hard data. One of Brunei's stronger scores. Maintain and ensure data is regularly updated in UNESCO.
indicators.brunei_challenge: Ensure data timeliness; functional literacy vs formal literacy distinction

indicators.name: Workforce Diversity
indicators.source_name: WEF EOS — Diversity and Inclusion Index
indicators.submission_notes: Survey. Complex policy challenge. Immigration reform and inclusive workplace policies would help.
indicators.brunei_challenge: Bruneianisation policy limits workforce diversity perception; limited international talent integration
